toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties folder

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties folder

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize it.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **`ToolsVersion` attribute** and **XML namespace** on `<Project>` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `<Import>` of Microsoft.Common.props and Microsoft.CSharp.targets** — handled by the SDK
3. **Configuration/Platform PropertyGroups** with Debug/Release settings — these are SDK defaults
4. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`OutputType`**, **`RootNamespace`**, **`AssemblyName`**, **`FileAlignment`**, **`AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`**, and **`Deterministic`** — all SDK defaults that can be removed
6. **`TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2`** — needs to become `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>`
7. The Debug/Release property groups are redundant since the SDK handles these configurations automatically
8. **Framework assembly references** like System and System.Core are implicit in SDK-style projects, so those explicit references can be dropped
9. **Explicit `<Compile Include>` items** — SDK-style uses implicit globbing to automatically include source files
10. **Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs** — this file becomes unnecessary since SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes from project properties instead

So the modernized project file will be much leaner. I'll convert it to SDK-style format and remove the AssemblyInfo.cs file entirely, letting the project properties handle those assembly attributes.

    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolArgs.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties folder

data.content:
Done. Here's what was modernized:

**Legacy patterns removed (47 → 10 lines):**

| Legacy Pattern | Modernization |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` attribute |
| Explicit `<Import>` of props/targets | Handled by SDK automatically |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| Debug/Release PropertyGroups | All values are SDK defaults |
| `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | Not needed (SDK defaults) |
| `OutputType Library` | SDK default for class libraries |
| `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` matching filename | Inferred by SDK |
| Explicit `<Compile Include>` items | Implicit globbing |
| Framework references (System, System.Core…) | Implicit in SDK-style |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Auto-generated from `<PropertyGroup>` metadata |

The final project file:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```
